name: Placental MSC EVs did not beat placebo in knee osteoarthritis
summary: A triple-blind placebo-controlled knee osteoarthritis trial found no clinical or MRI advantage from a single placental MSC-EV injection.
statement: In 31 patients with bilateral grade 2 or 3 knee osteoarthritis, a single intra-articular placental MSC-derived extracellular vesicle injection was safe but did not improve VAS, WOMAC, Lequesne, or MRI findings beyond saline placebo over 6 months.
population_or_model: Adults with bilateral grade 2 or 3 knee osteoarthritis
time_horizon: Single injection with 2- and 6-month clinical follow-up and 6-month MRI follow-up
quantitative_note: The trial analyzed 58 knees from 29 patients and detected no statistically significant between-group clinical or MRI differences.
caveats: This is disease-specific knee osteoarthritis evidence, not a broad aging trial.
A single EV dose and short follow-up may not test all EV regimens.
The null result is important because it is placebo-controlled and directly tests functional and imaging outcomes.
